learning_outcomes.description: identify, analyse and integrate relevant industry legislation, codes and standards,
identify, analyse and integrate appropriate structural, construction and environmental strategies,
collaboratively produce a schematic design proposal,
individually produce a design development report, and
collaboratively produce a set of construction drawings.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale: 

Students work in a small group to resolve and develop a provided schematic design proposal, including the selection of structural materials and set outs, service strategies and envelope systems and materials based on environmental performance requirement and the requirements of the National Construction Code. Groups must also prepare and submit weekly meeting minutes to track project progress and guide engagement with tutors. The collected meeting minutes are to be submitted with Assignment 1. Group peer assessment tools (BuddyCheck) will provide formative feedback on group performance in Assignment 1 that modifies individual student marks.

Group work note:

Group work plays a significant part of BLDG3220, and has been incorporated into the subject to help prepare you for working in architectural practice should you decide to take a year out between the Bachelor and Masters courses. Exercises in week 1 of semester will help to form groups and to make clear agreements or contracts between group members about how to communicate, how to share resources, how to give feedback and advice to other group members and the consequences for any group member for not following the group agreement. Group work can be difficult, particularly as the stresses of semester mount and assessment items for other subjects are due. If you find that your group is not functioning effectively please contact the course co-ordinator for support.

Complex / authentic assessment using AI and/or MT to support learning: This task has been designed to be challenging, authentic and complex. Whilst students may use AI and/or MT technologies, successful completion of assessment in this course will require students to critically engage in specific contexts and tasks for which artificial intelligence will provide only limited support and guidance. A failure to reference generative AI or MT use may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct. To pass this assessment, students will be required to demonstrate detailed comprehension of their written submission independent of AI and MT tools.
assessments.submission_guidelines: The School of Architecture, Design and Planning uses Blackboard and Turnitin for assessment submission. Turnitin is accessed through the course Blackboard site. Turnitin also checks for plagiarism or instances where the original work of others is not appropriately acknowledged. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Uploaded files must contain readable text and not be rasterised. Students are advised to commence assignment uploads with sufficient amount of time (consider possible technical problems with computers, internet speed, etc). After successfully submitting an assignment through Turnitin, a ‘Submission Complete!’ screen will be displayed. It is the student’s responsibility to check assignment preview and confirm successful submission. If the ‘Submission Complete!’ screen isn’t displayed, the student should regard the submission as unsuccessful. Students should download a copy of the digital receipt as proof they have submitted the assignment. Students who are experiencing upload issues must advise the Course Coordinator immediately by email and should include screenshots and a copy of the assessment for submission. To meet professional accreditation, public engagement and quality assurance obligations, digital copies of all course assessment items must be submitted in addition to any hard copy submission requirements specified in individual Course / Studio Outlines. Any physical models should be photographed and a minimum of two photographs describing the complete and full model must be included in the digital submission.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: The late penalty for this assessment item will be calculated as follows:
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

In Assignment 2a students continue to work with their group from Assignment 1 to further resolve the schematic design proposal, this time at a finer level of detail. This is graded individually, but students continue to work in alignment with the rest of their group to resolve construction details at 1:20, 1:10 or 1:5, in a way that supports and reinforces the architectural ideas that were presented in Assignment 1.

Assignment 2a is the in-class process work that will form the basis of Assignment 2b, and students will be given tasks at the start of the class which are to be completed in draft form and submitted to the course coordinator at the close of class.

Assignment 2a is a pass/fail assessment, Identity Verified Assessment Hurdle (IVAH).

In-person assessment prohibits use of AI or MT: This assessment task is to be completed in-person. The use of generative Artificial Intelligence (AI) or Machine Translation (MT) tools will not be permitted. Any attempted use of AI or MT may constitute student misconduct under the Student Code of Conduct.
assessments.submission_guidelines: Students are to submit physical drawings (on paper or trace) to the course coordinator at the end of class. Ensure your name is on your submission.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.
assessments.late_submission: The late penalty for this assessment item will be calculated as follows:
assessments.additional_info: You cannot defer or apply for an extension for this assessment.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale: In Assignment 2b students refine and complete the work started in classes during weeks 5, 6, 7 and 8 which form Assignment 2a. Assignment 2b is graded individually, but students continue to work in alignment with the rest of their group , to resolve construction details at 1:20, 1:10 or 1:5 in a way that supports and reinforces the architectural ideas that were presented in Assignment 1. A Detail Development template will be provided as a guide.

assessments.task_description:
Assessment Rationale:

In Assignment 3 students return to working collaboratively with their group from assignments 1 and 2 to produce a set of coordinated and coherent construction drawings for the design they have developed and refined in Assignments 1 and 2.

Groups must also prepare and submit weekly meeting minutes to track project progress and guide engagement with tutors. The collected meeting minutes are to be submitted with Assignment 3. Group peer assessment tools (BuddyCheck) will provide summative feedback on group performance in Assignment 3 that modifies individual student marks.

learning_activities.topic:
Field trips

Weekly in-class workshops will be supported by site visits to view and examine buildings under construction and completed buildings.

Currently three site visits are being planned:

  • the Advanced Engineering Building
  • the Global Change Institute
  • 25 King Street, Fortitude Valley

Dates for site visits will be confirmed as soon as possible. Site visits will be planned for Thursday afternoons.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
